mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows during rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.04).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, with t-stat < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which fuels consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 0.4 percent MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and real GDP confirms synchronized supply and demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Synchronized growth in industrial production and GDP fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales figures indicate weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances during late-cycle phases.
